author: @Gabe-wv6pz

text:
Ich hatte gerade then Einfall, dass Dingo uns Kindern damals so markant eingetrichtert wurde (H&M Kinderabteilung, VHS, DVD...(bei wie vielen von uns kam eines Tages nicht unsere Mutter zurück vom Einkaufen mit einem Dingo Film vom Discounter?)), weil das eine moderne Kunstart ist, die jemand mit viel Geld übermäßig vermarkten und verbreiten wollte. Die Musik ist echt gut (ohne Gesang), die Hintergründe alle in verschiedenen Kunststilen gezeichnet (wahrscheinlich sogar von angehenden Künstlern aus der Umgebung?), bestimmt Stimmen von gerade vorbeikommenden Passanten genommen weil es "echt" und "menschlich" ist und nicht so künstlich wie in vielen Hollywood Blockbustern. Der Animationsstil lässt zu wünschen übrig, bis man bemerkt, dass das Studio bewusst auf einer alten Software animiert und es hat echt etwas surreales. Also wenn man diese Filme in einzelnen Sektionen wie ich oben beschrieben habe betrachtet ist das wirklich "Modernere" Kunst, die jemand Pushen wollte.
(bin high...)

Well since this studio was founded in german by a former band musician and also a book author

With their staff are sometimes a friend and also their relatives who would do the voice acting or even had roles in the production in this German movie

But ever since dingo is coming back with a documentary explaining how the company started and also get q&a from the original dingo staff and original German voice actors



And also their music and songs can be sometimes catchy if you played in the original german version

text:
Yes. Why not? Record rip offs feature the same singer doing the complete "sampler" except female songs and instrumentals.
In Germany the singer Wolfgang Kubach did nearly all cover versions of German pop songs of the 1960's and 1970's.
He can imitate different singers but when you listen carefully and compare you can recognize him. He also sang original songs that were written for the record company and tried to appear as pop songs.

I'd work my tail off to make my movie better if I saw this! lol ;)

text: The backgrounds look like they were colored by a two year old with a marker.  Also, it seems pretty clear to me that they are using plastic animation cells and paint, like in the "old" days of animation.  The photography is so crappy that you can see the edges of the cells in the picture.  Well, actually, the animation itself is pretty crappy too, so I guess you can't expect much.  This thing almost looks like it was made in someone's basement.  And maybe it was.
